title: The Little Turtle Who Never Stopped Trying

illustrations.alt: Toby the turtle stands at the starting line of the Forest Race while other animals laugh at him
Toby practices climbing hills while Mrs. Owl watches from a branch and cheers him on
Toby crosses the finish line of the Forest Race as all the animals cheer for him

moral: Winning isn't about being the fastest — it's about never giving up. Perseverance always finishes the race.
moralZh: 胜利不在于跑得最快——而在于永不放弃。坚持总能完成比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Deep in Sunbeam Forest, where the trees stood tall like green umbrellas and the stream sang a bubbly song all day long, there lived a little turtle named Toby.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Toby had a bright green shell with golden spots, four stubby legs, and the biggest, dreamiest eyes in the whole forest. And he had a dream.

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "I'm going to win the Forest Race this year! I just know it!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Every year, the animals of Sunbeam Forest gathered for the Great Forest Race. The winner got the Golden Acorn Trophy and a whole year's worth of bragging rights. Ollie the Otter had won last year — and the year before that. He was fast and sleek and very, very proud.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Did I just hear someone say they're going to win the race? Let me guess — was it a mushroom? Because mushrooms move faster than turtles! HAHAHA!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ollie the Otter laughed so hard he nearly fell off the log he was sitting on. Skip the Squirrel covered his mouth. A few other animals giggled too.

paragraphs.speaker: Skip
paragraphs.text: "Come on, Toby. Turtles can't run. You're better off picking berries or something. The race is for the fast ones."

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "But I've been practicing! I've been walking around the pond every morning!"

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Walking around the pond? Oh wow! At that speed, you might finish the race by next winter! Come on, everyone — the real fun starts at the starting line!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ollie bounded away, and the other animals followed. Toby watched them go. His big dreamy eyes suddenly looked a little watery. He sat down slowly and stared at his stubby feet.

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Maybe they're right. Maybe I'm too slow. Maybe I shouldn't even try..."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: A soft rustle came from the oak tree above. Mrs. Owl, the wisest creature in the forest, fluttered down and landed gently beside Toby. She had speckled feathers, round amber eyes, and a voice as warm as a mug of cocoa.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Did I hear someone saying they shouldn't try? That sounds like the saddest song I've ever heard."

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Mrs. Owl! I want to win the Forest Race, but everyone says turtles are too slow. And... and they're right. I AM slow."

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Toby, let me tell you a secret. Winning a race isn't about being the fastest. It's about who keeps going when everyone else has stopped. Do you know what that's called?"

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Um... never giving up?"

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Exactly! It's called perseverance. And perseverance always, ALWAYS finishes the race. Will you try?"

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "I'll try! I'll try my very best!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And so Toby began to train. Every single morning, before the sun was even fully awake, Toby would crawl out of his cozy shell-bed and start moving.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Day One: Toby walked from his rock to the big oak tree. It took him forty-five minutes. He was so tired he fell asleep under a daisy.

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Phew! That was... that was a lot. But I did it! One step at a time!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Day Seven: Toby walked from his rock to the big oak tree AND all the way to the stream. It took him an hour and a half. His legs wobbled like jelly, but he didn't stop.

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Keep going, Toby. Slow and steady. Slow and steady!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Day Fourteen: Toby crawled up Sunny Hill for the first time. He slipped on a wet leaf. He tripped over a pebble. He rolled backward twice. But he reached the top. Mrs. Owl was waiting for him with a juicy berry.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Owl
paragraphs.text: "Well done, Toby! You climbed a hill! That's something Ollie has never done — because he has never had to try so hard. Do you see? Every step makes you stronger."

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "It DOES feel good! My legs are wobbly, but my heart feels REALLY strong!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Finally, race day arrived. The forest meadow was bursting with animals. There were banners, balloons, and a giant Golden Acorn Trophy glistening in the sun.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Well, well, well! Look who actually showed up! The Tiny Tortoise! Ready to come in last place?"

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "I'm ready to FINISH the race. That's what matters."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ollie blinked. Toby's answer surprised him — but he just scoffed and turned away. The race master, Mr. Badger, raised his flag.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Badger
paragraphs.text: "Ready, set... GO!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: WHOOOOSH! The animals tore off like a rainbow blur. Ollie the Otter zoomed ahead, his sleek body cutting through the air. Skip the Squirrel bounced from stone to stone. Benny the Bunny was a fluffy white rocket.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Toby? Toby took one slow step. Then another. Then another. He didn't look at the other racers. He looked at the path ahead.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Pretty soon, the fast animals were far ahead. Ollie was already halfway through the course. But then something happened. The course went up Rocky Ridge — a steep, bumpy hill. Ollie, who had never trained on hills, started to slow down. His legs ached. His lungs burned.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Oof! This hill is... is... too hard! I need a break!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Ollie sat down under a bush. Skip and Benny soon joined him, panting heavily. They had never practiced hills either.

paragraphs.speaker: Skip
paragraphs.text: "Let's just... wait a minute... I can't feel my legs..."

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And while they rested, a small green turtle with golden spots was slowly, steadily climbing Rocky Ridge. Step by step. One foot in front of the other.

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Slow... and... steady... I've done this before... I can do it again!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Toby passed Ollie, who was still catching his breath under the bush. Ollie's jaw dropped.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Wait — WHAT?! How is the turtle passing me?!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Toby didn't stop. He reached the top of Rocky Ridge, and then — joy of joys — he saw the finish line. All the animals who had already finished gasped. They couldn't believe their eyes.

paragraphs.speaker: Crowd
paragraphs.text: "It's Toby! Toby the turtle! He's actually going to FINISH!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Toby crossed the finish line with one final, determined step. The crowd erupted into cheers — not just for the winner, who had finished long ago, but for Toby. Because he had done something nobody thought he could do.

paragraphs.speaker: Mr. Badger
paragraphs.text: "And here comes Toby the Turtle! He didn't come in first — but he NEVER gave up! Let's hear it for Toby!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: The whole meadow cheered. Mrs. Owl hooted the loudest of all. Toby puffed out his little chest — as much as a turtle can — and smiled the biggest smile of his life.

paragraphs.speaker: Mrs. Owl
paragraphs.text: "You see, Toby? You didn't win the trophy — but you won something BETTER. You proved that perseverance always finishes the race."

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "It wasn't easy. But I kept going! One step at a time!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Later that day, Ollie the Otter shuffled over to Toby. He looked — for the first time ever — humble.

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Toby... I'm sorry I laughed at you. You were slow, yeah — but you were BRAVE. You never stopped. That's... that's pretty amazing."

paragraphs.speaker: Toby
paragraphs.text: "Thanks, Ollie. Maybe next year you can train with me? We could practice hills together!"

paragraphs.speaker: Ollie
paragraphs.text: "Heh. Deal. But I'm still faster!"

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And so, Toby the Turtle didn't win the Golden Acorn Trophy. But he won something far more precious: the respect of every animal in Sunbeam Forest, and a heart full of pride that no trophy could ever replace.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: So if you ever feel like you're too slow, or too small, or not good enough — remember Toby. It doesn't matter how fast you go. What matters is that you never, ever stop trying. Because perseverance always finishes the race.
